phases.goal: Discover your medium and establish a daily creative habit

steps.title: Pick one creative medium
steps.description: Choose a single medium to focus on for the next 12 weeks — drawing, writing, music, photography, or another craft. Breadth kills momentum at the start. Commit to one.
steps.success_metric: One medium chosen and written down

steps.title: Start a 10-minute daily practice
steps.description: Creativity is a muscle. Commit to 10 uninterrupted minutes every day in your chosen medium. No quality bar — just showing up. Use a habit tracker app to log streaks.

steps.title: Consume to inspire
steps.description: Study three creators in your medium whose work you admire. Analyse what makes their work distinctive. Write one sentence per creator summarising their 'signature'.

phases.goal: Deepen fundamentals and produce your first finished piece

steps.title: Take a structured course or class
steps.description: Enrol in one focused course — online or in-person — to build core technique. Prioritise fundamentals: drawing basics, music theory, narrative structure, or composition depending on your medium.
steps.success_metric: Course enrolled and at least 50% completed

steps.title: Complete one finished piece
steps.description: Ship something — a drawing, a short story, a song demo, a photo series. 'Done' beats 'perfect'. The goal is to experience the full creative cycle from blank canvas to finished output.

steps.title: Join a creative community
steps.description: Find a community of practitioners — a local class, an online forum, or a weekly meetup. Sharing work and receiving feedback accelerates growth faster than solo practice.
steps.success_metric: Community joined and at least one piece shared for feedback

phases.goal: Build a small portfolio and share your work publicly

steps.title: Create a small portfolio
steps.description: Curate 3–5 of your best pieces into a simple portfolio — a folder, a free website, or a social profile. The act of curating forces you to reflect on your growth and define your voice.

steps.title: Share publicly and collect feedback
steps.description: Post your work to at least one public channel — Instagram, Behance, SoundCloud, a writing platform, or a local gallery. Real-world feedback from strangers is irreplaceable.
steps.success_metric: Work shared publicly and at least 3 pieces of external feedback received
